| Rank | Ask | Times Asked | Status | Effort | Fix Sketch |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Collapsible card layout for panel features** | 3x | ❌ | M | Replace flat section list in popup with `<details>`/card pattern; token success auto-collapses token card and promotes it to bottom |
| 2 | **Mod panel 90% width** | 3x+ | ❌ | S | CSS: `.pop-token, .pop-section, .pop-stats { width: 90%; margin: 0 auto; }` in popup.css; also set `popup.html body { min-width: 400px; }` if not already |
| 3 | **AUTH persistence (no re-entry between sessions)** | 3x | ⚠ | S | The `gam_pending_invite` local backup shipped in v10.0; but the core issue is `chrome.storage.session` being evicted — verify `_persistRotatedToken` writes local FIRST then promotes to RAM (HOLE-S3 in UAT_TOKENS); also `__validateModAuth` uses raw fetch bypassing SW vault (HOLE-S5) |
| 4 | **BAN: team macro selection not propagating to ban message body** | 2x | VERIFY | S | `modtools.js:8180` does `body.value = opt.dataset.body || ''` — body IS populated. Probable cause: macro selected BEFORE body textarea renders (race). Add `defer` or `requestAnimationFrame` before write. |
| 5 | **Force Rehydrate — analyze, remove duplicate, or fix** | 2x | ⚠ | S | Duplicate removed in v10.0 (AGENT_BRIEF: `#maintRehydrateAlias deleted`). Fix: when `gam_settings.workerModToken` empty, change button label to "Open Tokens tab" and route there instead of attempting hydrate |
| 6 | **Rename "Triage Console" -> "MANAGE /USERS"** | 1x | ❌ | S | Find and replace in modtools.js: `Triage Console` -> `MANAGE /USERS` at lines 8957, 12168, 15384, 16112, 19963, 20103; popup.html:90 also |
| 7 | **FIRST RUN SETUP: don't show for catsfive; collapse on success; restart button** | 1x | ⚠ | S | Wizard exists at popup.js:1768 and DOES hide when token present (`hasToken` check L1786). MISSING: (a) collapse-to-summary rather than full hide on success (b) "Restart onboarding" button for already-authed users who need to re-claim |
| 8 | **Maintenance buttons 4x2 grid (not single column)** | 2x | ❌ | S | Add CSS to popup.css: `.pop-maint { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:6px; }` for the user-tier buttons. Lead-tier maint stays single-column (fewer buttons). |
| 9 | **FIREHOSE: extract features from firehose data** | 2x | ❌ | L | Three concrete items: (1) auto-populate AI tard suggester from firehose usernames [G2 in handoff]; (2) brigade detector — flag novel-account ratio >30% in thread; (3) pattern Discovery nightly clusters. | 10 | **Stats panel: clickable data (drill-down)** | 1x | ✅ | - | Ships since v9.4.5. `popup.html:41-68` data-drill cards. `popup.js:2627-2958` drill renderer. Evidence: six cards each with `data-drill` attr and `addEventListener('click')` at popup.js:2948 |
| 11 | **Stats data persistence: use D1, NOT local** | 1x | ❌ | L | All 6 stat-card values (`s-pending`, `s-dr`, `s-banned`, `s-today`, `s-msgs`, `s-notes`) are populated from `chrome.storage.local` (roster + log arrays). No D1 endpoint backs these. Fix: new worker endpoint `/mod/stats` that queries D1 actions table + mod_tokens + gaw_users and returns the 6 counts. Popup calls it on load. |
| 12 | **"DRILL //" verbiage — meaningless. Explain or remove** | 1x | ❌ | S | `modtools.js:16972-16973` CSS `::before` pseudo-element injects `"DRILL // "`. Remove the `::before` block or replace content with a more descriptive label like `"DETAIL VIEW"` |
| 13 | **User info hover: cut off by browser boundaries** | 1x | ✅ | - | `modtools.js:9473-9494` — viewport-clamp logic exists (`Math.max(MARGIN, Math.min(...))`). VERIFY: code shows proper bounds clamping with flip logic. If Commander still sees clipping, the MARGIN constant (likely 8px) may need to be 12px. |
| 14 | **Remove highlighting feature (grayed-out features visible)** | 1x | ❌ | S | Not found in code — need Commander to identify which specific "highlight" feature he means. Best candidates: (a) SUS overlay yellow pills on usernames (b) the "compact bylines" feature flags visible but greyed. Clarify before touching. |
| 15 | **Export CSV — what's it for?** | 1x | ⚠ | S | `popup.html:84` and `popup.js:2922-2957`: Export CSV exports the currently-viewed drill-down table (bans, DR queue, etc.) to clipboard as CSV. It works. The ask is really "explain it" — add a `title=` attribute: `title="Download the current drill-down table as CSV (clipboard)"` |

---

## C. ASKS BY DOMAIN

### C.1 Auth & Tokens

**AUTH CARRY-OVER [ASKED 3x]** — Commander repeatedly gets asked to re-enter creds. The wizard (v9.21.0) suppresses for existing users. The real hole is `_persistRotatedToken` at background.js:1010 — it writes RAM FIRST then storage. If SW evicts between those two operations, the new token is gone. Fix is to write storage first, verify, then promote to RAM (UAT_TOKENS HOLE-S3).

**FIRST RUN SETUP for catsfive** [ASKED 1x] — ✅ wizard skips when token present (popup.js:1784). MISSING: success-state collapses to summary rather than disappearing, and no restart button.

**Force Rehydrate** [ASKED 2x, "BEST PART IS NO PART"] — Duplicate button removed in v10.0. The surviving button at popup.html:115 still misbehaves when there is no token (hydrate is a no-op). Fix: route to Tokens tab instead when token absent.

**Token menu order for lead** [ASKED 1x] — lead token section appears BELOW the team token section in the DOM. For lead-authenticated users, lead token inputs should float top, team token inputs below, or be reorganized into a single "Credentials" card.

### C.2 Panel Organization

**Collapsible cards** [ASKED 3x] — Nothing found in popup.html/js using `<details>` or collapsible card pattern for the main popup sections. The modtools.js sidebar HAS a `makeCollapsibleSection` helper at line 11662 but it is NOT used in the popup. Fix: port that pattern to popup sections.

**90% width mod panel** [ASKED 3x+] — popup.css shows the panel width is determined by the browser popup default (360px). No `width: 90%` found anywhere in the popup styling. Fix is a one-liner in popup.css.

**Token menu to bottom after auth** [ASKED 2x] — After successful `is_lead` whoami (popup.js:695), the DOM order stays unchanged. The token section should reorder or collapse.

**Maintenance buttons grid** [ASKED 2x] — Single-column `.pop-maint-row` layout confirmed in popup.html:150-228. No grid layout on the maint section. Two-column grid is a CSS-only fix.

**Stats panel ONLY on Stats tab** [ASKED 1x] — Stats cards ARE inside the Stats tab visually, but the `div.pop-stats` block sits ABOVE the tab nav in popup.html:43-68, before the `nav.pop-tabnav` at line 34. The cards are always visible regardless of active tab because `wireTabNav` (popup.js:1937) maps the stats tab to `['.pop-stats', ...]` but the stats div is outside the tab-gated structure. This is the bug — the stats block renders outside of any tab section.

### C.3 Workflow / UX Labels

**Rename "Triage Console"** [ASKED 1x] — String found in 7 locations in modtools.js and popup.html:90. None have been updated.

**"DRILL //"** [ASKED 1x] — CSS pseudo-element at modtools.js:16972-16980 injects the literal string "DRILL // " before every drill-down. Not informative. Remove or replace.

**Export CSV purpose** [ASKED 1x] — Feature works (popup.js:2922). Needs tooltip clarification only.

**"Highlight" feature** [ASKED 1x] — Cannot confirm from code alone what specific element Commander means. No `highlight` or `grayed` feature flag found in modtools.js SETTINGS object. Needs clarification from Commander before touching.

### C.4 Ban Pipeline

**Macro not propagating to ban body** [ASKED 2x] — Code at modtools.js:8180 does write to body, but this is in the Message tab, not the Ban tab. Ban-tab macro handler at modtools.js:7280 calls `rpcCall('macroUse')` but does NOT inspect the response body to pre-fill `#mc-ban-msg`. The `__add__` path at 7177-7193 uses `window.prompt()` — a UX trap flagged in UAT_BANS. The data flows in for `mm_reply` macros in the Message tab, but ban macro body propagation to the ban textarea is the missing wire.

**Custom-modified ban response remembered for session** [ASKED 2x] — Zero session memory found. No `sessionStorage` usage in the ban modal path. Straight-forward to add.

### C.5 Discord + Rotation

**ROTATION FEATURE: DM each mod via Discord + ZIP attachment + PS1 unzip wrapper** [ASKED 1x] — Partial foundation exists: `popup.js:856` `__dmTemplate()` generates a DM text block and copy button. BUT: (a) auto-send to Discord requires a bot webhook per-channel, which is not implemented; (b) ZIP attachment via Discord webhook requires `multipart/form-data` — not built; (c) PS1 unzip wrapper script does not exist. This is an L-effort net-new feature set.

**Multi-lead delegation** [ASKED 1x] — Not in code. Worker `requireLeadAuth` at gaw-mod-proxy-v2.js:591 is binary (is_lead true/false). A `is_senior` tier would require schema migration + worker helper update + popup gate change.

### C.6 Stats + Data

**Stats only on Stats tab** [ASKED 1x] — Bug confirmed: `div.pop-stats` at popup.html:43-68 is outside the tab-gated sections. It always shows. Move it inside the Stats tab section wrapper.

**Stats clickable** [ASKED 1x] — ✅ Delivered. `data-drill` cards since v9.4.5. popup.html:41-86 full drill-down machinery. Evidence: `popup.js:2948 stats.addEventListener('click')`.

**Stats D1 persistence** [ASKED 1x] — ❌ All stat values come from `chrome.storage.local`. Pending DR, banned, and bans24 counts are derived from the local roster array, not D1. No `/mod/stats` worker endpoint found.

### C.7 Firehose

**FIREHOSE: build features that use the data** [ASKED 2x] — The firehose stores posts + comments in D1 (FTS5 index, gaw_posts + gaw_comments). Currently NO popup or content-script feature queries this data for mod decisions beyond the sticky-detect scan. Three under-leveraged capabilities: (1) tard/sus pattern suggester queries last 80 usernames via firehose but likely runs empty because firehose.active is opt-in; (2) no brigade detector; (3) modmail AI history-augment only queries `mod_modmail_responses`, not the user's post/comment firehose history.

### C.8 Bugs

**BAN: macro not propagating** [ASKED 2x] — See C.4. The ban-tab macro select populates use_count but does NOT write selected body to `#mc-ban-msg` textarea.

**BAN: custom response remembered** [ASKED 2x] — See C.4. Not implemented.

**User info hover bounds clipping** [ASKED 1x] — Viewport clamping code EXISTS at modtools.js:9473-9494. Either the MARGIN constant is too small (try 12px) or the clamp fires for position but not max-height, causing vertical overflow. Check `tooltipEl` max-height CSS.

9. **C12 Modmail macros dropdown in Message tab** — Matrix says ❌. AGENT_BRIEF v10.2 says "Discovered A2 was already shipped v9.8.0; matrix was stale." Update to ✅ with evidence cite `modtools.js:8180 mm_reply macro handler`
10. **Worker version** — Update worker from `v9.4.6` to `v9.4.7`

## F. NOTES FOR CTO

### F.1 The 90% width issue is a process indictment

Commander has asked for `90% width` at least three times. The change is `width: 90%; margin: 0 auto` in popup.css — one line. That it has never been attempted suggests the agents are pattern-matching to features rather than reading Commander's literal words. This audit found zero CSS in popup.css resembling `90%` or `width: 90`. Ship this in the next 10 minutes.

### F.2 Firehose data is sitting idle

The firehose (D1 + gaw_posts + gaw_comments + FTS5) stores every post and comment seen. The ONLY features that query it today are: sticky-detect scan (modmail only) and the firehose panel UI. The tard suggester fires against `gaw_users` (usernames only), not post content. The modmail AI only queries `mod_modmail_responses`. None of the content-based signals Commander identified ("Brigade detector", "pattern Discovery") are built. Commander called this out explicitly: "FIREHOSE: data sitting there, leverage not extracted." This is the largest single unrealized value pool in the codebase.

### F.3 Stats cards have a tab visibility bug

`div.pop-stats` in popup.html is at line 43 — above the tab nav at line 34. The `wireTabNav` function maps the stats tab to show `.pop-stats`, but because the stats block is rendered unconditionally in the HTML before the nav, it is ALWAYS visible. The stat cards bleed outside the Stats tab. This needs to be wrapped in a `data-tab-section="stats"` container that wireTabNav controls.

### F.4 Auth carry-over root cause

`_persistRotatedToken` in background.js:1010 promotes to `secretCache` RAM before confirming storage write. SW eviction between those two lines = token in RAM only = next page load has no token = re-auth required. Fix: write + verify storage FIRST (3 retries already exist), THEN assign `secretCache.workerModToken`. This is UAT_TOKENS HOLE-S3, confirmed HIGH priority.

### F.5 Ban-tab macro body wiring is the missing link

The ban-tab dropdown at modtools.js:7280 fires `rpcCall('macroUse', { id })` which bumps use_count. It does NOT set the `#mc-ban-msg` textarea body. The code at 8180 that sets `body.value = opt.dataset.body` is in the MESSAGE tab. Two separate handlers, one missing write. This is the bug Commander is hitting with "team macro selection didn't propagate to ban message body."

### F.6 What shipped in v10.0-10.2 that addresses current asks

Already delivered (do not re-ship):
- Brave Shields detection + amber rescue banner (v10.0)
- `gam_pending_invite` chrome.storage.local backup (v10.0)
- Force re-hydrate duplicate removed (v10.0)
- Token field 3-dimension differentiation (v10.0)
- Bar-icon tooltip Y-gap 6px -> 14px (v10.1)
- Maintenance pareto cut: 4 user buttons visible, 6 dev-jargon in `<details>` (v10.1)
- Two-mods-same-machine collision guard (v10.1)
- Dock toggle emoji -> text + 4s undo toast (v10.2)
- Auth-fail banner "Open ModTools popup" button (v10.2)
- A2 modmail macros in Message tab confirmed delivered v9.8.0 (v10.2 discovery)

### F.7 The stats D1 ask is architectural

Stats persistence in D1 requires a new worker endpoint plus a migration if cross-mod stats are needed. This is not a popup change — it is an L-effort that touches worker + D1 schema. Commander's ask is specifically "persistent across versions" — meaning local Chrome storage wiped on reinstall should not reset counts. D1 is correct for this. Recommend scheduling for the next dedicated worker-side session.

---

**Word count: ~4,100**
